title: Use explicit train-to-test importance weighting for domain shift
summary: The dataset has obvious shift candidates such as Pre-Season Testing and changing year/race mixes.
rationale: The dataset has obvious shift candidates such as Pre-Season Testing and changing year/race mixes. Covariate-shift literature says importance weighting is most useful when the learner is misspecified, which is likely here because the pipeline is a hand-engineered tabular approximation of a tactical process.
implementation_hint: Estimate train-to-test density ratios with a simple train-vs-test classifier or Kernel Mean Matching on leakage-safe current-lap features. Cap extreme weights, and test three variants: full weighting, downweighting Pre-Season Testing only, and separate GP vs testing experts blended by OOF AUC.
expected_effect: Small but real lift if current CV is optimistic because training distribution differs from the 2025-ish test mix.
risk: High-variance weights can hurt more than help; clip weights aggressively and report effective sample size alongside CV.
